red_baseline.reason: T017 owns the official-SDK real-process MCP initialization, schema projection, call/error, framing, concurrency, output isolation, interruption/restart, and scope-refusal contract; T018 owns the server implementation.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED: all six official-SDK scenarios reach the real spawned Node process and fail with MCP connection closed because the T018-owned src/mcp/stdio-server.js entrypoint is absent.

validation.summary: The official SDK helper and all three real-wire suites parse successfully.
0/6 pass; every scenario fails at the official SDK connection because the real server entrypoint is absent, with no direct Kernel or handler substitute.
The T018-owned real stdio entrypoint is confirmed absent.
Syntax check passed for 88 JavaScript files.
All T017 helper and protocol contract changes passed whitespace validation.

review.evidence: The tests use official Client plus StdioClientTransport against a spawned Node entrypoint, capture raw stdout without replacing the SDK lifecycle, require all 18 closed schemas and normalized tool/domain errors, exercise concurrent lifecycle calls and adapter restart, assert child-output framing, and prove Plugin cwd cannot become scope.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Real-wire tests require MCP results and lifecycle state to match existing CLI-visible registry/run/journal authorities; no test-only MCP backend is permitted.
The suite covers local stdio only, low-risk positive mutation, medium-risk refusal, and excludes force/risky/raw/remote inputs.
Evidence is explicitly scoped to the source stdio server, current Node/OS test cell, and real official-SDK process boundary; it does not claim Plugin or other-OS readiness.
All six tests start the real server entrypoint with official SDK transport and make no direct Application Kernel or handler calls.
Tool listing and calls require bounded closed schemas; output-isolation tests prohibit non-JSON-RPC stdout lines.
Project operations use an explicit registered alias, while the Plugin-cwd case omits projectRef and must return typed scope_not_resolved.
A nested force input must return normalized input_invalid with req_ identity, no journal, and no effects.
Tool annotations are asserted as hints only; medium risk and scope/refusal behavior must still be decided by Kernel results.
The adapter restart scenario requires a managed run to survive MCP close and reappear with the same run ID and one spawn receipt.
Raw captured stdout must consist exclusively of parseable JSON-RPC frames; child stdout/stderr is asserted only within the structured tool response.
T017 records only source-server MCP evidence and cannot fill shipped bundle, Plugin, host, update, uninstall, recovery, or other-OS cells.
Real MCP lifecycle results are compared to existing CLI process state/run identity after adapter close, allowing only protocol provenance differences.
The official client exercises initialized lifecycle, list/call, concurrent requests, close, and a fresh restart; tests do not treat source inspection as protocol evidence.
